raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-001",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights remains a scaffold with all articles (I–VI) undefined. Without a human-authored BoR, Arbiter cannot enforce scope boundaries, the enterprise perimeter is absent, and the entire compliance architecture lacks a constitutional foundation. This leaves the DIO in a default-permissive state with no prohibited activities, no core identity, and no human authority reservation.",
      "msp_section": "Relationship to the Bill of Rights (MSP subordinate to BoR)",
      "bor_article": "Articles I, II, III, IV, V, VI (all undefined)",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 5,
      "priority": 125,
      "rationale": "Critical × Urgency × Impact = 5×5×5=125. The BoR is the supreme governance document. Without it, no operational rule, SLA, or constraint can be meaningfully anchored to a defined enterprise scope, rendering all other operational gaps secondary."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-002",
      "description": "The MSP Rules section is empty (bootstrap scaffold). No operational SLAs, organ availability targets, runtime targets, or cadence baselines are defined. Nomos evaluates Action Proposals using this empty ruleset, effectively operating in default-permissive mode with no machine-actionable operational constraints.",
      "msp_section": "Rules (none — bootstrap seed)",
      "bor_article": "None directly; constitutional void prevents rule anchoring",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 4,
      "impact": 4,
      "priority": 80,
      "rationale": "Criticality 5 because all AP adjudication passes without operational standards. Urgency 4 because it must follow BoR authorship. Impact 4 because the system currently authorizes actions without availability, latency, or cadence checks, risking operational degradation."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-003",
      "description": "Observed spine-transition patterns show repeated, rapid toggling of multiple organs between ALIVE and DEGRADED states (approx. 30-second cycles). No stability criteria, anti-flapping hysteresis, or acceptable state-change frequency are defined. The MSP does not specify runtime targets for recovery or thresholds for declaring persistent DEGRADED status, leading to uncontrolled state oscillations.",
      "msp_section": "Purpose (translates BoR into machine-actionable safety rules); Rules (none)",
      "bor_article": "Article II (Prohibited Activities, undefined) — would normally bound acceptable system behaviour",
      "criticality": 4,
      "urgency": 4,
      "impact": 3,
      "priority": 48,
      "rationale": "Criticality 4 because flapping could cause erroneous AP blocking or resource exhaustion. Urgency 4 because the flapping is actively occurring in the recent spine slice. Impact 3 because it currently affects reliability but may not immediately violate undefined boundaries."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-004",
      "description": "The 'Recent Goals' list is empty, indicating no operational improvement or cadence goals have been dispatched. There is no active operational feedback loop to address gaps, enforce baselines, or drive the system toward defined operational targets. This absence contravenes the MSP's intended purpose of maintaining a machine-actionable operational constitution with amendment and evolution.",
      "msp_section": "Amendment Protocol (implies active maintenance via PEM cycle); Purpose (operational constitution)",
      "bor_article": "Article I (Scope of Enterprise, undefined); Article IV (Human Authority Reservation, undefined)",
      "criticality": 3,
      "urgency": 2,
      "impact": 3,
      "priority": 18,
      "rationale": "Criticality 3 because operational stagnation does not immediately break functionality. Urgency 2 because the bootstrap is recent and no acute breach exists. Impact 3 because missing goals delay the transition from permissive to governed operation."
    }
  ]
}
